CONTENT.Career: Bauthéac scored his first goal for Brisbane Roar in an A-League match against Melbourne City. He left the Roar after the 2018–19 season.On 25 July 2019, he signed a two-year contract with Omonia Nicosia. He made his debut on 24 August 2019 against Doxa Katokopias in the 2019–20 season premiere and scored the second goal of the match with a direct free kick.
CONTENT.Honours: OmoniaCypriot First Division: 2020–21Cypriot Cup: 2021–22Cypriot Super Cup: 2021
CONTENT.External links: Eric Bauthéac – French league stats at LFP – also available in French (archived)Éric Bauthéac at L'Équipe Football (in French)

SUMMARY:
Éric Bauthéac (born 24 August 1987) is a retired French professional footballer who played as an attacking midfielder.
He has also played in France for Saint-Étienne, Lille, Dijon, Nice and Cannes.
